title: Number of Subsequences That Satisfy the Given Sum Condition
hints: Sort the array first. Once sorted, the minimum and maximum of any subsequence are simply its first and last elements in the sorted order.
Use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end. If the sum of elements at these pointers exceeds the target, move the right pointer left.
If the sum is valid, count how many non-empty subsequences can be formed using the left element as min and the right as max. Remember that elements between them can either be included or excluded.
